sources_exist.note: All 11 canonical HTTP(S) citation occurrences resolve to identifiable pages.

figures_match_sources.note: Only the DRaaS forecast and reduced reliance on specialist teams are supported in context. The remaining citations do not establish the score-driving 18% adoption, 10-year analog, 38% owner age, 8 consolidators, exact 4.5x/9.0x multiples, or the stated 541519 billing mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The declared fractions and pi values are bounded, but the assumed occupational remapping and automatable fractions depend on an unverified assertion that installation/support shops dominate this residual code. t50_years=8 is derived from unsupported current-adoption and analog endpoints. ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, owners_60plus_pct, t50_years) is unresolved.

rubric_consistent.note: The non-null dataset inputs match exactly and formulas follow the frozen rubric, but the catch-all rule is not met: the run names candidate subsegments without sourced top-three revenue shares or evidence that installation/support/resale is dominant. M also uses MSP asking ranges, including an SDE tier, rather than normalized actual 541519 transaction evidence. BORDERLINE: |S - pass cut 3.00| = 0.1403 is unresolved because these score-affecting gaps could change the classification. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S, V is reviewed and is not independently disqualifying because the flagged deltas are within tolerance.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift and no-proxy statements are structurally responsive, but the terminal and pricing checks state an unsupported dominant-subsegment conclusion and unsupported 40%/25%/20%/15% revenue shares. The no-proxy statement also presents 18% as Kaseya-derived even though Kaseya reports future intent, not current material adoption.

source_audits.note: The page verifies seven active MSP platforms, 169 disclosed 2025 deals and 69% PE participation. It says classification is primarily 541512 with 541519 secondary for cybersecurity-led MSPs; it does not support eight platforms actively acquiring the dominant 541519 installation/support pool.

Breakwater gives a 4x-5.5x EBITDA range for owner-operated MSPs with 50-70% MRR and a 3x-4x SDE range for mostly break-fix firms. It does not report a 4.5x median actually paid for normalized NAICS 541519 targets.

Kaseya supports 33% predicting future AI use, 85% calling automation a must-have, and 37% expecting implementation challenges. It does not measure current material adoption or support the run's estimated 18% for 541519 firms.
The page supplies a 7x-10x+ range for platform-ready MSPs with $3 million-plus EBITDA, not a 9.0x observed exit multiple for $10 million-plus EBITDA NAICS 541519 platforms.

The page says 33% of enterprises ran more than half their workloads in cloud in 2021. It does not establish near-zero or 10% penetration in 2012, 50% in 2022, adoption among small IT shops, or an 8-10 year zero-touch MDM cycle.

U.S. Bank supports that more than half of U.S. small-business owners are over 55 and only 54% have formal succession plans. It does not provide the cited ABS age-bin figures or support a 38% age-60-plus estimate for 541519.

Breakwater supports that break-fix and one-time projects are valued below MRR. It does not say T&M is the dominant model, quantify the 541519 revenue mix, or establish that AI savings are passed directly to clients.

The accessible page is a report landing page and does not expose evidence for the asserted recurring-versus-project dynamics or the 541519 billing shares. A title/download invitation is insufficient support.

BetterCloud describes SaaS zero-touch workflows and self-healing security automation, but the cited 42% of automation leaders/51-75% of help-desk tickets figure is absent, and the page does not quantify displacement of 541519 installation revenue.

The page forecasts the global DRaaS market rising from about $16.1 billion in 2025 to $46.1 billion in 2032 at 16.2% CAGR and says automated orchestration reduces reliance on specialized recovery teams, supporting the cited DRaaS displacement mechanism.

Breakwater supports the quoted comparison of an 80% MRR MSP with one at 40% MRR/60% project work. It does not establish that 541519 revenue is 40% T&M, 25% break-fix, 20% retainer and 15% resale, nor the stated 13% and 46% margins.

reasons: The catch-all rule is not satisfied at reviewer_note, inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, and cross_checks.terminal_value: the run provides no sourced top-three revenue subsegments or revenue shares proving that installation/support/resale is dominant. Re-run after establishing the top-three 541519 revenue subsegments from comparable evidence, and score the evidenced dominant segment.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.kaseya.com/press-release/kaseyas-2024-msp-benchmark-report-finds-that-cybersecurity-is-a-top-revenue-driver-for-three-quarters-of-managed-service-providers/), future AI-use intent is converted to an unsupported 18% current-adoption estimate. Supply a current material-use measure or a fully supported ESTIMATE chain for the selected dominant subsegment.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.zippia.com/advice/cloud-adoption-statistics/), the claimed 2012-to-2022 10%-to-50% small-IT-shop adoption path is absent. Re-run t50_years from documented, comparable analog endpoints.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/private-equity-msp-2026/), seven primarily 541512 MSP platforms are turned into eight buyers for 541519 without named qualifying evidence. Identify each PE-backed platform and a qualifying recent add-on in the selected exact-industry pool.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.breakwaterma.com/blog/msp-it-services-valuation-multiples-2026), advisory ranges for MSP profiles are treated as exact 541519 transaction inputs, and the break-fix tier uses SDE without the required owner-compensation normalization. Supply actual normalized target and platform transaction evidence for the selected dominant subsegment.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url and inputs.pi_dist.sources[0] (https://www.breakwaterma.com/blog/msp-it-services-valuation-multiples-2026), the record invents a 40%/25%/20%/15% 541519 billing mix from a generic MRR-versus-project illustration. Supply evidence for the dominant segment's revenue mix and AI-efficiency pass-through before reassessing pi_dist.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://ir.usbank.com/news-events/news/news-details/2025/U-S--Bank-Small-Business-Survey-Finds-More-Than-One-Third-of-Gen-Z-and-Millennial-Owners-Plan-to-Acquire-a-Business-From-a-Retiring-Owner--But-Many-Older-Owners-Arent-Ready/default.aspx), general over-55 and succession facts do not establish 38% age 60-plus in 541519. Provide an industry-specific measure or a complete proxy derivation using cited age bins.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, owners_60plus_pct, t50_years)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S, V
